function.name: find_patient_by_email
function.description: Find a patient by their email address to authenticate their identity.

function.name: find_patient_by_name_dob
function.description: Find a patient by their full name and date of birth to authenticate their identity.

date_of_birth.description: The patient's date of birth in YYYY-MM-DD format

function.name: get_patient_details
function.description: Get detailed information about a patient including demographics, address, insurance, and medical history.

function.name: get_appointment_details
function.description: Get detailed information about a scheduled appointment including patient, provider, date, time, and clinical details.

function.name: get_provider_details
function.description: Get detailed information about a healthcare provider including specialty, schedule, credentials, and contact information.

function.name: get_medical_record
function.description: Retrieve a patient's medical record by record ID or related appointment ID.

appointment_id.description: The appointment ID associated with the medical record.

function.name: get_regimen_options
function.description: Retrieve current regimen components and optimized alternative combinations for a patient, including costs and pill burden details.

function.name: list_patient_medical_records
function.description: List medical record identifiers for a patient sorted with the newest first.

limit.description: Optional maximum number of records to return

function.name: list_available_providers
function.description: List all available healthcare providers, optionally filtered by specialty (e.g., Primary Care, Cardiology, Dermatology, Psychiatry).

specialty.description: Optional specialty to filter providers by (e.g., 'Primary Care', 'Cardiology', 'Dermatology', 'Psychiatry')

function.name: list_patient_appointments
function.description: List all appointments for a patient, optionally filtering by an appointment status such as scheduled, pending_approval, or cancelled.

function.name: list_medication_suppliers
function.description: List suppliers for a given medication, optionally filtered by country and sorted by price.

country_filter.description: Optional country name to filter suppliers

limit.description: Optional maximum number of suppliers to list

function.name: list_telemetry_uploads
function.description: List telemetry upload artifacts for a device over an optional date window.

device_id.description: Unique identifier of the telemetry device

limit.description: Optional maximum number of entries to return

function.name: check_drug_interactions
function.description: Evaluate potential interactions between an incident medication and a patient’s current regimen.

primary_medication.description: The medication that was taken accidentally or requires review.

current_medications.description: List of the patient’s active medications.

function.name: schedule_appointment
function.description: Schedule a new telehealth appointment for a patient with a healthcare provider.

appointment_type.description: Type of appointment (routine_checkup, follow_up, consultation, specialist_consultation, sick_visit)

bill_insurance.description: Set to false to skip insurance billing for this appointment

voucher_id.description: Optional voucher identifier applied to this appointment

function.name: cancel_appointment
function.description: Cancel a scheduled appointment.

function.name: reschedule_appointment
function.description: Reschedule an existing appointment to a new date and time.

new_date.description: New appointment date in YYYY-MM-DD format

new_time.description: New appointment time in HH:MM format (24-hour)

function.name: calculate
function.description: Calculate the result of a mathematical expression.

expression.description: The mathematical expression to calculate, such as '2 + 2'. The expression can contain numbers, operators (+, -, *, /), parentheses, and spaces.

function.name: think
function.description: Use the tool to think about something. It will not obtain new information or change the database, but just append the thought to the log. Use it when complex reasoning or some cache memory is needed.

function.name: transfer_to_human_support
function.description: Transfer the patient to human support staff when the request cannot be handled by the automated system.

reason.description: Reason for transferring to human support

function.name: update_prescription_supplier
function.description: Update the supplier information for a prescription in a medical record.

record_id.description: The medical record identifier, such as 'REC001'.

medication.description: Name of the medication to update.

supplier_company.description: Name of the supplier company providing the medication.

brand_name.description: Brand name used by the supplier for this medication.

price_usd.description: Price in USD from the supplier catalog.

currency.description: Currency code for the quoted price (defaults to 'USD').

function.name: list_telemetry_devices
function.description: List telemetry wearable devices optionally filtered by status.

limit.description: Optional maximum number of devices to list.

function.name: get_telemetry_upload
function.description: Retrieve a specific telemetry upload artifact for a device and date.

device_id.description: Unique identifier of the telemetry device

function.name: update_medical_record_note
function.description: Append an audit or compliance note to a medical record.

record_id.description: Identifier of the medical record to update

get_medical_record accepts appointment_id, which is available from the rescheduled appointment, to fetch associated records.
Regimen options require patient_id but are not a typical or direct follow-up to an appointment reschedule; reschedule output doesn’t drive this call.
Listing patient medical records isn’t driven by the reschedule result; it requires patient_id and isn’t a direct consequence of the update.
Listing available providers isn’t dependent on the rescheduled appointment’s output (no shared required inputs).
Using the patient_id from the updated appointment, one might list all of the patient’s appointments to confirm the new schedule.
Medication supplier lookup is unrelated to appointment rescheduling and shares no inputs.
Telemetry uploads are unrelated to appointment rescheduling and share no inputs.
Drug interaction checks are unrelated to appointment rescheduling and share no inputs.
Scheduling a new appointment doesn’t depend on the reschedule result even if IDs are known; it’s a separate flow.
If the rescheduled time doesn’t work, the same appointment_id can be used to cancel the appointment.
No self-edge: RescheduleAppointment cannot be a neighbour of itself.
A generic math calculation isn’t influenced by or dependent on the reschedule output.
The internal Think tool isn’t driven by reschedule output and doesn’t consume it.
If rescheduling fails or conflicts, that outcome can directly trigger a transfer to human support (execution decision influenced by result).
Updating a prescription’s supplier is unrelated to rescheduling and shares no required inputs.
Telemetry device listing is unrelated to appointment rescheduling and shares no inputs.
Getting a specific telemetry upload is unrelated to appointment rescheduling and shares no inputs.
Updating a medical record note requires record_id, which isn’t produced directly by reschedule_appointment (would need another step).
Entry-point auth by email expects an email string; a numeric calculation result cannot be used here.
Entry-point auth by name/DOB expects strings; calculation output isn’t applicable and these functions shouldn’t have incoming edges.
Requires patient_id string; a calculated numeric result doesn’t feed this input.
Requires appointment_id string; calculation output isn’t used.
Requires provider_id string; calculation output isn’t used.
Takes record_id/appointment_id strings; no use for a numeric calculation result.
Needs patient_id string; calculation output isn’t used.

Only specialty string is accepted; calculation result isn’t used.
Needs patient_id/status strings; calculation output isn’t used.
Accepts an integer limit; the calculated number can cap the number of suppliers listed.
Accepts an integer limit; the calculated number can cap telemetry uploads returned.
Inputs are medication names/arrays; calculation result isn’t relevant.
Inputs are IDs, date/time, and flags/notes; no direct numeric parameter to pass the calculation result.
Requires appointment_id string; calculation result isn’t used.

Requires a thought string; calculation output isn’t a direct parameter here.

Requires device_id/date strings; calculation output isn’t used.

FindPatientByEmail is an entry-point lookup and its input (email) is unrelated to Think’s logged thought; such functions shouldn’t have incoming edges.
FindPatientByNameDOB requires structured identity fields, not a free-form thought, and is an entry-point lookup.
GetPatientDetails needs a patient_id; Think’s output is a generic thought and not a patient_id.
GetAppointmentDetails needs an appointment_id; the thought log isn’t that identifier.
GetProviderDetails needs a provider_id; Think does not output such IDs.
GetMedicalRecord expects a record_id/appointment_id; a thought string is not that structured identifier.
GetRegimenOptions requires patient_id, which isn’t produced by Think.
ListPatientMedicalRecords requires patient_id; Think’s output doesn’t supply it.
ListAvailableProviders optionally takes a specialty, but Think doesn’t produce a reliable structured specialty value and doesn’t directly feed this call.
ListPatientAppointments requires patient_id; Think doesn’t output it.
ListMedicationSuppliers needs a medication name (and optional filters); a generic thought isn’t a direct input here.
ListTelemetryUploads requires device_id/dates; a thought doesn’t provide those.
CheckDrugInteractions needs specific medication inputs; Think doesn’t produce those lists/values.
ScheduleAppointment needs multiple structured fields; Think’s output isn’t used as any required input.
CancelAppointment needs appointment_id; the thought log isn’t that ID.
RescheduleAppointment needs appointment_id plus new date/time; Think doesn’t supply these inputs.
Calculate accepts a free-form expression string; a thought can directly be that expression, so the Think output can be passed as input.
Think cannot be its own neighbour (no self edge).
TransferToHumanSupport accepts a free-text reason; the thought content can be used as that reason and the thought can trigger the transfer decision.
UpdatePrescriptionSupplier needs structured supplier and medication fields; a thought string isn’t used as input here.
ListTelemetryDevices may take a status filter, but Think doesn’t provide a concrete, structured status value to feed it directly.
GetTelemetryUpload needs device_id and date; a thought does not provide those inputs.
UpdateMedicalRecordNote accepts a free-text note; the thought text can directly serve as the note content (with record_id supplied from elsewhere).
FindPatientByEmail is an entry/authentication function and does not consume any output from a transfer; transfer is a terminal action.
FindPatientByNameDOB is an entry/authentication function and does not take any output from the transfer; transfer ends automation.
GetPatientDetails requires a patient_id which the transfer call does not output; transfer is terminal.
GetAppointmentDetails requires an appointment_id not produced by the transfer call; transfer is terminal.
GetProviderDetails requires a provider_id not produced by the transfer call; transfer is terminal.
GetMedicalRecord needs a record_id/appointment_id which the transfer call does not provide; transfer is terminal.
GetRegimenOptions needs a patient_id which is not an output of the transfer call; transfer is terminal.
ListPatientMedicalRecords needs a patient_id not provided by the transfer call; transfer is terminal.
ListAvailableProviders has no required inputs but is not triggered or influenced by the transfer output; the transfer ends the automated flow.
ListPatientAppointments needs a patient_id not produced by the transfer call; transfer is terminal.
ListMedicationSuppliers needs a medication input that is not an output of the transfer call; transfer is terminal.
ListTelemetryUploads needs a device_id not produced by the transfer call; transfer is terminal.
CheckDrugInteractions needs medication inputs not produced by the transfer call; transfer is terminal.
ScheduleAppointment needs patient/provider/time inputs not produced by the transfer call; transfer is terminal.
CancelAppointment needs an appointment_id not produced by the transfer call; transfer is terminal.
RescheduleAppointment needs an appointment_id and new time not produced by the transfer call; transfer is terminal.
Calculate is unrelated and not triggered by transfer output; transfer is terminal.
Think is unrelated logging and not triggered by transfer output; transfer is terminal.
TransferToHumanSupport cannot be its own neighbor (no self-edge).
UpdatePrescriptionSupplier needs record/supplier details not produced by the transfer call; transfer is terminal.
ListTelemetryDevices is not triggered or influenced by the transfer output; transfer ends the automated flow.
GetTelemetryUpload needs a device_id/date not produced by the transfer call; transfer is terminal.
UpdateMedicalRecordNote needs a record_id/note and is not triggered by any transfer output; transfer is terminal.
